phase3_findings.description: The _disk non-empty precondition is vacuous by construction: printf is a builtin and "$@" always carries the literal members, so _disk is non-empty on every path and the '' arm is unreachable — the guard renders a green result for a condition it never tests, and its failure text names causes (ls absent, glob matched nothing) it structurally cannot observe.

The cross-row overlap `case` interpolates the member into a case PATTERN, so a member containing a glob metacharacter is read as a pattern rather than a literal. The registry does carry such members (skills/review/phases/*.md appears verbatim on budget-watch-missing lines), so the shape is one row-schema change from live.

The cross-row overlap detector's positive (reporting) branch is dead — driven only by the real tree, where the rows are disjoint by construction, so an inverted test or broken sentinel would stay permanently green.

The registry-derived roster is extracted from budget-watch lines only; a budget row whose members are ALL absent emits budget-watch-missing lines exclusively and so never enters the roster, letting the coverage equality pass while that row goes unchecked.

is_budget_row's docstring describes a counterfactual failure mode neither keying produces: the shipped keying makes a command-less non-budget row False at BOTH sites, and the rejected proxy makes it True at both — the 'opposite directions' split describes neither. Conclusion right, mechanism false against HEAD.

The registry-invariant probe passes vacuously on an empty (or lazily-populated) ROWS: bad is [], the probe prints nothing, and the assertion passes having checked zero rows. It is the only new assertion whose whole population comes from inside Python and the only one without a non-empty precondition.

The registry invariant pins argv-vs-watch_literals but not `record`/`watch_globs`, which budget_row and watch_list also consume — a budget row missing `record` satisfies the pin and then raises KeyError at use (fail-closed, but as a traceback rather than a named breadcrumb).
A5i3 pins the new row's glob leg; its literal leg is still unpinned — the one place a watch_literals copy-paste from the sibling row would go undetected.
A5o prose says 'UNREADABLE module registry' while the fixture plants absence (rm -f); defensible since arm 8 renders the same text for both, but the reproduction guidance misleads.

The inventory's Registry-surface cell cites `#624 registry invariant`, which is a section-header comment rather than an assertion name; every other cell cites arm labels that also prefix assertion names, so this entry silently changes referent class.
The inventory's Budget-rows arm list is a hand-maintained enumeration (a mirror-fact that rots) and already omitted A5i2.
A6d/row-comment provenance claims carry a bare count ('proved this drift twice').
